message:
conformance: regenerate fixtures, reports, and snapshot JSON

Bulk regeneration of every committed fixture and per-family
conformance report under `tests/conformance/` after the
multi-contract fixture schema change that ships in sibling
commits (expected_output / expected_output_bytes /
expected_output_pattern / expected_return / expected_values /
expected_n_value). The scripts that drive this output now run
cleanly against the new schema, so every artifact they emit is
re-written from scratch.

Files touched:

- `tests/conformance/fixtures/io_internal_ops.json` — rename of
  a single case (`IO_adjust_column_tab_aligns` →
  `IO_adjust_column_tab_is_ordinary`) whose `expected_output`
  flipped from "8" to "2" because the underlying
  `_IO_adjust_column` no longer treats TAB as an alignment marker;
  it is now a plain character advancing the column by 1. The new
  case name matches what the generator produces, and the new
  `expected_output` matches the current glibc behaviour we are
  asserting parity against (verified by a direct printf walk).
- `tests/conformance/fixtures/printf_conformance.json` —
  descriptive renames of the %g / %f variants
  (`sprintf_g_basic` → `sprintf_g_small_fixed`,
  `sprintf_f_very_small` → `sprintf_f_subnanosecond_precision`,
  etc.) with identical input/output — readability pass only.
- `tests/conformance/fixtures/pthread_thread.json` — schema
  refresh for pthread_* fixtures that started using the new
  `expected_return` / `expected_values` contract for entry points
  where the meaningful result is carried out-of-band in an
  out-pointer rather than the return value.
- Every `*.v1.json` per-family summary (callthrough_census,
  closure_sweep_report, conformance_coverage_snapshot,
  cross_report_consistency, errno_edge_report,
  feature_parity_drift_diagnostics, feature_parity_gap_bead_coverage,
  feature_parity_gap_bead_ledger, fixture_pipeline, fixture_unit_tests,
  fuzz_harness_architecture, fuzz_membrane_validation,
  fuzz_phase1_targets, fuzz_phase2_targets, hard_parts_truth_table,
  mode_semantics_matrix, per_symbol_fixture_tests,
  perf_regression_attribution, perf_regression_prevention,
  posix_obligation_matrix, replacement_profile,
  replacement_zero_unapproved_fixtures, reverse_round_contracts,
  runtime_env_inventory, stub_census, stub_todo_debt_census,
  support_matrix_maintenance_report, symbol_latency_baseline,
  verification_matrix) — regenerated from the generator scripts.
  The only semantic deltas visible in diff are:
    (a) the new timestamp / commit-sha provenance fields,
    (b) the cases/fields that moved under the new
        `per_snapshot` / `closure.*` / `attribution_summary`
        groupings,
    (c) counts/tallies that updated naturally as sibling commits
        landed new ABI entries and closed more beads.
- `feature_parity_gap_bead_dashboard.v1.md` — the human-readable
  dashboard rebuilt from the ledger JSON, so everything lines up.

None of the changes lower any threshold or drop any previously-
enforced assertion. This is the committed artifact side of the
refactor whose code/script side landed in earlier commits;
checking these in alongside keeps CI green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
